channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Free condoms, lube and sex ed materials in stock for all ages at CA libraries, thanks to Planned Parenthood
item.description: The Sacramento Public Library system adding condoms, lube and other sex resources in a dozen of its libraries in partnership with Planned Parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump targets ‘Anonymous’ author and former top cybersecurity official in escalation of retribution
item.description: President Donald Trump is intensifying his efforts to punish his critics by signing a pair of memoranda directing the Justice Department to investigate two officials from his first administration and stripping them of any security clearances they may have.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's 'buy' tip on social media before his tariffs pause made money for investors who listened
item.description: President Donald Trump offered some financial advice on Wednesday morning that ended up making a lot of money for any lucky investor who listened to him.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department sharply criticizes judge who blocked enforcement of an order targeting a law firm
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is sharply criticizing a judge who halted enforcement of a White House executive order meant to punish a prominent law firm, continuing a pattern of Trump administration attacks on the judiciary over decisions that officials don't like.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP holdouts threaten Trump’s ‘big, beautiful’ budget bill as Speaker Johnson postpones vote
item.description: House Republicans have abruptly postponed a late evening vote on their budget framework as grave misgivings from conservative GOP lawmakers threw it in flux.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minnesota rolls out dashboard tracking federal funding cuts in the state
item.description: As of Wednesday, the site reported roughly $296 million in federal grants to Minnesota had been cut and millions more expected to be cancelled.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump replaces Patel with Army secretary as ATF director
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: New poll reveals Trump's approval ratings
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: White House rolls out implementation of AI for federal employee records
item.description: The U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) will post an updated Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) at the close of business Wednesday that paves the way for a major artificial intelligence rollout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges temporarily block 5 Venezuelans' deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: Federal judges in New York and Texas temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting five Venezuelan men it alleges are gang members by using the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: FEMA to revoke $188M in New York City migrant grants
item.description: The money is part of the Shelter and Services Program, which helps local governments provide for migrants.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: US trade representative testifies before House on Trump trade agenda
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer testified before the House Ways and Means Committee on Wednesday morning on President Trump's trade agenda, as the latest reciprocal tariffs took effect overnight.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump should focus on ‘targeted tariffs’: Rep. James Comer
item.description: Comer also emphasized the importance of considering what must be produced in the United States from a national security perspective.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Biden believes he would've beaten Trump in election: Author
item.description: Author Chris Whipple released "Uncharted: How Trump Beat Biden, Harris, and the Odds in the Wildest Campaign in History" on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge ordered the White House on Tuesday to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events, affirming on First Amendment grounds that the government cannot punish the news organization for the content of its speech. U.S. District Judge Trevor N.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amy Coney Barrett is what is needed in Supreme Court justice: Analyst
item.description: Barrett has sided with liberal justices on multiple hot-button issues, including DOGE regulation and Arizona’s voter ID law.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Weber, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota pushes back on Trump demand to end DEI or lose school funding
item.description: Minnesota Education Commissioner Willie Jett told federal officials the state already complies with federal civil rights laws and that the Trump administration did not have the power “to unilaterally overrule the will of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration: U of M student in ICE custody should remain jailed
item.description: The Trump administration says a University of Minnesota graduate student from Turkey should remain jailed while the Department of Homeland Security moves forward with removal proceedings. That's according to court documents filed just ahead of a deadline late Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stocks are volatile as selloffs sweep through global markets
item.description: Shares nosedived around the world Monday as higher U.S. tariffs and a backlash from Beijing triggered massive sell-offs. Trump has defended the tariffs: “You have to take medicine to fix something.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Dow jumps 1,300 points at open, rebounding amid China tariff tension
item.description: Many are waiting to see if the nation’s three major indices take any hits from the escalating trade tension between China and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Growing opposition from House conservatives threatens to derail Trump's agenda
item.description: Growing opposition among hard-line House conservatives to the Senate’s framework for advancing President Trump’s ambitious legislative agenda is threatening to make this week’s vote one of the heaviest lifts yet for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block order to return Maryland man from El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens more tariffs on China as global markets plunge
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering additional tariffs on China. The Republican president's threat came Monday after Beijing retaliated against his announcement of tariffs last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
